source_rationale: Adiponectin is a canonical adipokine secreted exclusively by adipocytes, widely used as a definitive adipocyte marker
Perilipin-1 coats lipid droplets in mature adipocytes and is a hallmark of terminal adipocyte differentiation
PPARγ is the master transcription factor driving adipogenesis and maintaining adipocyte identity
Lipoprotein lipase is highly expressed in adipocytes to hydrolyze circulating triglycerides for fatty acid uptake
Glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase is essential for triglyceride synthesis in adipocytes
ATGL (adipose triglyceride lipase) is the rate-limiting enzyme for lipolysis in adipocytes
Hormone-sensitive lipase is a key lipolytic enzyme characteristic of mature adipocytes
CD36/fatty acid translocase mediates long-chain fatty acid uptake in adipocytes
Aquaporin-7 is an adipocyte-specific glycerol channel important for lipid metabolism
Glycerol-3-phosphate dehydrogenase is a classic marker of adipocyte differentiation
FOS is an immediate-early gene suggesting this adipocyte subtype is in an activated or stress-responsive state
TWIST1 is a transcription factor involved in adipocyte precursor regulation and brown/beige adipocyte identity
Annexin A1 is upregulated in this adipocyte subtype, involved in anti-inflammatory signaling in adipose tissue
Collagen XV alpha-1 marks this adipocyte subtype with extracellular matrix remodeling activity
FKBP5 is a glucocorticoid-responsive gene marking stress-responsive adipocytes
Fatty acid binding protein 4 is a canonical adipocyte marker highly expressed in this subtype
Aldehyde dehydrogenase 1A1 marks visceral-type adipocytes involved in retinaldehyde metabolism
Pyruvate dehydrogenase kinase 4 is upregulated in adipocytes during fasting/metabolic adaptation
Intelectin-1 (omentin) is an adipokine secreted by visceral adipose tissue
ABC transporter A8 is involved in lipid transport and marks this adipocyte subpopulation
CIDEA is a lipid droplet-associated protein enriched in thermogenic/beige adipocytes
